turns.content: did you hear about the new BMA regulations for street food vendors?? 😤 I am SO stressed na ka. city hall announced new rules and all the vendors in my area are panicking
Bangkok street food regulations always cause a stir. What are they proposing this time? New permit rules or restrictions on where vendors can set up?

3 new rules na ka. first — all cart must have stainless steel surface, no more wood. second — fire extinguisher at every cart. third — cooking area must be at least 2 metres from the walkway edge. the first two I can do but 2 METRES?? my soi is not that wide!! if I move back 2 metres I am in the middle of the road na ka

The fire extinguisher is totally reasonable — that's basic safety and they're cheap. Stainless steel surface is more of a cost but doable. The 2-metre setback is the real problem though. That could make it physically impossible to operate in many soi. What happens if vendors can't comply — fines?

fine is 2,000 baht first time and 5,000 baht second time. and if they shut you down for one week that is like 60,000 baht lost revenue for me!! the noodle uncle next to my spot say he will just ignore it but I don't want to risk it na ka. I need my license

Right, ignoring it is risky with those fines and potential shutdowns at your volume. Are the vendors in your area organizing at all? Sometimes a collective pushback through the vendor association gets BMA to soften the rules or extend the timeline.

yes there is a meeting next week with the district office!! the vendor association is going to push back on the 2 metre rule because it's impossible for most soi. I think they will negotiate to 1.5 metres or maybe 1 metre with some kind of safety barrier. the stainless steel and fire extinguisher we just accept na ka — those are fair. but the distance rule will kill half the vendors in Bangkok

Going to that meeting is important — strength in numbers. In the meantime, you could get ahead on the things you can control: upgrade your cart surface to stainless steel and buy the fire extinguisher now. That way if inspectors come, you're compliant on 2 out of 3 and showing good faith. How much would the stainless steel upgrade cost?

maybe 3,000-4,000 baht for the surface plus 800 baht for the fire extinguisher. about 5,000 baht total na ka. I just do it and show them I am a responsible vendor who follow rules. then hopefully the distance rule get changed at the meeting. ahhh Bangkok na ka always something new to worry about 😂 okay thank you for helping me calm down. time to go yell in my vendor group chat haha 🙏

Go rally the troops! And remember, every time there's been a big street food crackdown in Bangkok, the rules usually get softened after pushback. The city literally runs on street food — they know they can't shut everyone down. Get those upgrades done, show up to the meeting, and you'll be fine. 🙏

you are right na ka. Bangkok without street food is NOT Bangkok haha. I read that street food contribute like 300 billion baht to the economy every year!! they cannot kill that. I feel more confident now. going to order the fire extinguisher on Lazada tonight — saw one for 650 baht with good reviews 🔥

Exactly — street food is literally part of Bangkok's identity. And smart move ordering the extinguisher already. You'll be ahead of most vendors who will panic-buy the week before inspections start. Just make sure it's the right type for kitchen/cooking oil fires — look for a dry chemical or wet chemical one, not just the basic CO2 type.

arai wa there are different types?? okay I look for the kitchen one na ka. see this is why I ask you first haha. okay I go do my research now. thank you for always keeping me calm when Bangkok try to stress me out 😂🙏

Haha, Bangkok will always find new ways to stress vendors out — that's just how it goes! But you handle it every time. Get the right extinguisher, get the stainless steel quote, and show up to that meeting ready to fight for your soi space. You've got this! 🙏

I definitely got this na ka!! my cart is not going anywhere 😤 okay going to message the vendor group chat now and make sure everyone come to the meeting. strength in numbers like you say!! sawatdee ka 🙏

That's the spirit — rally the troops and show up strong! Your soi vendors are lucky to have someone proactive like you leading the charge. Good luck with the meeting, and let me know how it goes! Sawatdee ka! 🙏
